methods.description: Conduct a survival analysis to evaluate the impact of different diets on larval survival using Kaplan-Meier curves and log rank tests.

steps.description: Data from the experiment on the impact of diets on larval survival were transformed to count-time data.
steps.searchable_summary: transform survival data to count-time format

steps.description: Kaplan-Meier curves were used to compare diet-specific survival pattern for each group.
Comparison of survival rate in each larval diet to that of TetraMin Fish food was performed using log rank test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform log rank test for survival rate comparison

methods.description: Use ANOVA and other statistical tests to evaluate the impact of larval diets on various biological parameters.

steps.description: The Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) test was conducted to evaluate how larval diets impacted the mean pupation rate, adult emergence rate, and wing lengths.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ANOVA on pupation rate, emergence rate, and wing lengths

steps.description: Wald's test was used to determine significant differences between diets on the vectorial parameters of An. arabiensis based on χ2 and P-values.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Wald's test on vectorial parameters

methods.description: Collect and analyze pollen from various plants for use in feeding experiments.

steps.description: Pollen of T. latifolia was collected in the shore of the Lake Abaya, P. juliflora from the shrub dominated area of Lante, and the pollen of Z. mays L. from the farmland of Lemat.
The collected pollen was transferred to vials and sterilized within 24 h of collection using UV light overnight for 12 h and stored on silica gel until used for feeding experiments.
The C and N concentration of pollen diets were determined by Walkley-Black and Kjeldahl methods.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze C and N concentration in pollen

steps.description: The pollen grain sizes were measured according to Roulston et al.

methods.description: Collect and rear mosquito larvae for experimentation on the effects of different diets.

steps.description: Anopheles larvae were collected from the potential breeding habitats in the Arba Minch area.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Anopheles larvae from breeding habitats

steps.description: The larvae were reared in a controlled environment with specific temperature and humidity settings.
Larval development and survival were recorded twice a day.
